title: lobularia canariensis subsp. spathulata
intro: Lobularia canariensis subsp. spathulata, also known as sweet Alyssum or Canary Island sweet Alyssum, is a flowering plant species within the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lobularia canariensis subsp. spathulata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and support respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or tea, or in the form of tinctures or essential oils, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
